title: nepeta annua
intro: Nepeta annua, also known as annual catmint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Nepeta annua include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Nepeta annua are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
